topTargets.name: Jonah Coleman
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #10 RB3 Tier 2 (DEN), MFL ADP 20.19 — best RB likely to fall to 1.09 after Love and Price are gone. Roster has only 4 RBs vs 9 WRs, so RB is the clearest need and Coleman's Denver landing gives him a real touch path.

topTargets.name: Mike Washington Jr.
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #11 RB4 Tier 2 (LV), RSP DoT 82.5, ADP 24.09. Big-bodied LV back with 'better opportunity than ranking suggests' per Waldman — fills the RB hole if Coleman is taken at 1.07/1.08.

topTargets.name: Omar Cooper Jr.
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #12 WR6 Tier 2 (NYJ), RSP DoT 83.9, ADP 17.20 — three-board consensus Tier 2 talent. WR room is deep but Cooper as a Deebo-clone in NYJ is BPA insurance if both top RBs go ahead.

topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #13 RB5 Tier 2 (KC), RSP DoT 84.6 (RSP RB3 overall ahead of Coleman/Washington), ADP outside top 30. KC backfield + Waldman's Harold Fannin trajectory comp makes him the medium-RSP value play if board breaks for him.

topTargets.name: Kaytron Allen
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #14 RB6 Tier 2 (WAS). Pure positional-need fallback — by 1.09 if Coleman, Washington, and Johnson are all off the board, Allen keeps the RB-priority plan alive without reaching past Tier 2.

capPosture: Tight at $39.1M of $45M used ($5.9M space) with 5 expirings — can absorb the 1.09 active-roster cost but rounds 2-3 likely need to go taxi at 50% to preserve 20-man minimum and FA flexibility.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: Consensus #21 TE2 Tier 3 (PHI), RSP DoT 84.4. TE is a 2-deep need and Stowers as a converted-QB move-TE in Philly is a 2.09 home-run swing that fits a medium-RSP GM blending Waldman love with Consensus Tier-3 floor.

summary: Gridiron Geeks attack 1.09 with a clear RB-first mandate — 9 WRs vs 4 RBs makes Coleman/Washington/Johnson the target tree, all Consensus Tier 2 and all defensible under the 50/25/25 weighting. Rounds 2 and 3 lean taxi to protect the $5.9M cap cushion, with TE (Stowers) as the secondary need behind RB. Medium RSP affinity means no reaching past Consensus Tier 2 in round 1, but Waldman's grades break ties between similarly-ranked Tier-2 RBs.
